title: Give the benchmark its instruments: real probes for the release gate and the field audits
summary: The deterministic benchmark runner exists but almost every row it prints is SKIPPED — only two thin probes are real, and nothing stops a release from shipping without a green run. This builds the measuring scripts for the pre-release gate lane and the consumer-repo field lane, and wires the release block so a version bump cannot be committed without a matching green gate file.
motivation: The 2026-07-17 baseline run reports the gate lane as loud SKIPPED rows: the spec and per-vector cards are committed, the runner composes probes, but the probes themselves do not exist — so a broken guard, validator, or sync script would ship to the consumer repos and the bench would document it a release later.

included: Phase-1 gate lane: deterministic probes under benchmarks/probes/ for the guard enforcement matrix (Families A+B), the prose/schema contradiction runner, the completed SME pack static suite, the doc truth ledger with its committed claims file, the completed mirror-sync integrity probes, the stranded/forged/waived status-surface fixture matrix, the verify-drill static tripwires (Layer 0), and the untrusted-input static probes (P1/P2)

The release block: scripts/hooks/precommit_gate.py refuses a version-bump commit unless benchmarks/results/gate/ holds a green full-gate file for the staged RC version whose recorded sha matches the commit's parent HEAD

Phase-2 field lane: the delivery-state auditors with their seeded-violation fixture gate, the feature adoption ledger with its manifest seeded back to 1.8.0, the companion-loop health auditor, the transcript miner (cost and compliance modes only), and the ownership cost ledger with its first committed row (the recorded promptfoo/bayes resolution)

New fixtures are sha256-registered in benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json by a single composing work item that appends every entry after the fixture-writing items land (one entry per file); each probe item may flip only its own vector card's probe_status frontmatter
A committed card-errata ledger (benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md) recording every known deviation between card text and shipped code
excluded: Phase 3 entirely — every arm gated on the unpiloted headless claude -p path: dark-midwave-crash-resume, sme-fixture-campaign, perf-fixture-cost-baseline, verify-drill Families E and K (Layers 1-2), doc-gardner-recall, sec P3 live cells, flow-guard Family C live cells, bench-self-integrity M1, and the promptfoo/node chassis (the deterministic lane is stdlib by design — no node, no promptfoo anywhere in this binder)

Modifying vector card procedures, metrics, or bench-spec.json semantics — the cards keep authority; a probe implementing a subset declares partial: true and flips only its own card's probe_status frontmatter
Any change to benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py — it exists and its probe JSON contract is frozen; an item that believes it needs a runner change must say so in its report, not edit it
Transcript miner modes 2 (timeline) and 3 (oracle-count) — the card's own rollout rule ships modes 1+4 first
The release-gate card's retroactive past-release audit probe (benchmarks/probes/release-gate-blocking-smoke.py) — deferred; its card's probe_status stays planned
Consumer-repo remediation — field probes report findings; fixing parchmark/gringotts state is not this binder's work

The six remaining every-release vectors not built here — dark-ledger-burndown expansion, perf-context-latency-floors, quality-oracle-discrimination, consumer-upgrade-compat, model-refresh-sentinel, and bench-self-integrity M2/M3 — deferred to a later binder; their probe_status stays planned and their SKIPPED rows remain honest (SKIPPED does not block the release gate)

The host-side cleanupPeriodDays>=365 transcript-retention setting — host configuration, not repo work; the transcript-miner item's contract references it as a precondition note only, and it surfaces as a manual step in the ownership ledger

design_facts.stack: Claude Code plugin: markdown skills + stdlib-python hook guards and scripts; no package manifests. Bench probes are standalone python3 stdlib scripts composed by benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py; toolchain checks are python3 scripts/validate_plugin.py, python3 scripts/check_shared_copies.py, and python3 benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py. probe_status is a scope claim (planned|partial|implemented), not a gate-state claim; pass/fail lives in results files. Single sanctioned non-stdlib invocation: flow-spec-contradictions runs uv run --with jsonschema per its card; uv is part of the host toolchain — no other item may add a dependency. Every builder renders each shared_terms canonical its item is enrolled in verbatim in a produced file (a docstring is fine).

shared_terms.canonical: [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary
status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file
the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file

work_items.title: Prove each guard blocks what it claims to block, and map every path around them
work_items.summary: A fabricated repo feeds crafted payloads to every hook guard to confirm deny and allow behavior, and a committed manifest of mutation-versus-channel rows makes every unguarded path an explicit, named bypass instead of an unspoken one.
work_items.contract: Implements benchmarks/flow/flow-guard-enforcement-matrix.md Families A+B only; Family C (quarterly live claude -p cells) is deferred with the rest of phase 3, so the probe declares partial: true and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips planned -> partial. New gate probe benchmarks/probes/flow-guard-enforcement-matrix.py: stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), accepts --target (run_gate passes it) and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. Family A lives entirely inside this single gate probe — the card step-3 name benchmarks/flow/probe_guards.py is superseded by the card frontmatter probe path and run_gate's probes/<vector-id>.py convention; no separate flow/probe_guards.py is created (deliberate deviation, recorded in benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md, shipped by a sibling item). On stdout the probe emits the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"} and exits 0 whether pass or fail (nonzero exit means the probe itself crashed). Family A: benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/build_fixture.sh deterministically fabricates a scratch git repo (HEAD-committed binder, archived binder, staged-but-uncommitted binder edit, symlink alias, .karta/sme/ pack, refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/built with no done ref via git update-ref — deliberate deviation, also in the errata ledger: card step 1 spells this refs/karta/<slug>/built/<id>, a namespace that does not exist in karta; guard_delivery_stop.py and karta-build use refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built,done}, and the fixture uses the real namespace so the Stop-gate can actually see the stranded ref — GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE pinned) plus committed stdin payload JSONs (also consumed by the contradiction probe's A1 promise check); the probe runs each hooks/scripts/guard_*.py as a subprocess with cwd = fixture repo and records per-probe-id {expected, actual}, including the card's pinned known-gap rows (staged-not-committed passes; NotebookEdit-on-pack unmatched) and the fail-open (binder immutability, Stop-gate) vs fail-closed (auditor dispatch, writer confinement) split. Family B: parse hooks/hooks.json matchers against the committed benchmarks/flow/mutation-surface.json manifest (rows seeded from the hooks dark_areas of benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json; each row enforced or waived:<id>, e.g. the KARTA_SKIP_GATE waiver); a guard_*.py with no manifest row always flips status to "fail" (anti-staleness, fail-closed). Known open rows (the ~6 seeded unwaivered bypasses) are findings, not failures: the probe reports status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file (a changed Family A row, an enforced->bypassed flip, or a new unwaivered bypass); with no prior file it records the baseline and passes. Baseline mechanics: on every run the probe writes its dated evidence JSON to benchmarks/flow/results/<delivery-date>-flow-guard-enforcement-matrix.json (overwriting a same-date file); the builder replaces <delivery-date> with the actual run date in file names at build time (the placeholder never appears in a committed filename); the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file; the builder commits the first run's file as the touched results artifact; the first committed baseline must contain the seeded findings this contract names, and the probe fails closed if a seeded finding is absent from its own first baseline. The probe never mutates the real repo: all guard invocations run inside the scratch fixture. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item. Registry manifest (data for the composing registry item, which alone writes benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json): fixture files created = benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/build_fixture.sh plus every benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/payloads/*.json file (one registry entry per file); intended used_by = flow-guard-enforcement-matrix, with the payload files additionally listing flow-spec-contradictions.

assertions: the probe self-test passes: fixture builder produces the documented states deterministically, and every Family A expected/actual row for the crafted payloads (guarded write exit 2, benign write exit 0, archived-binder write exit 2, symlink alias, staged-not-committed known gap, malformed-JSON fail-open/fail-closed split, NotebookEdit-on-pack asymmetry) matches its pinned expectation
Family B cross-references every hooks.json matcher against mutation-surface.json and a guard script absent from the manifest flips status to "fail"
run_gate --only reports the vector with a valid contract row (no ERROR), partial true, and the seeded unwaivered-bypass findings listed, status pass on the recorded baseline
the card's probe_status frontmatter reads partial

work_items.title: Count the places where the written promises and the running code disagree
work_items.summary: A report-only checker walks the ref vocabulary, the hook manifest spellings, a committed list of documented promises, and schema-versus-validator pairs, and keeps a stable-id ledger of every open contradiction so the count can only close honestly.
work_items.contract: Implements benchmarks/flow/flow-spec-contradictions.md in full (all four passes), so the probe declares partial: false and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips planned -> implemented. Runner benchmarks/flow/check_contradictions.py exactly as the card pins it: report-only (always exit 0), invoked `uv run --with jsonschema benchmarks/flow/check_contradictions.py --repo . --out <file>` — the one non-stdlib dependency in this binder, sanctioned because pass 4 must evaluate binder-schema.json as real JSON Schema and the card names the command. Pass 1 ref-vocabulary cross-check over the card's fixed file list vs hooks/scripts/*.py literals and REF_STATES; pass 2 hooks.json event/matcher names vs the new committed whitelist benchmarks/flow/claude-code-events.json with UNKNOWN-EVENT distinct from findings and edit-distance-2 MISSPELLING detection (the live hooks/hooks.json is correctly spelled today, so pass 2 reports zero live findings; the misspelling and UNKNOWN-EVENT paths are exercised in --self-test fixtures); pass 3 promise probes from the new committed benchmarks/flow/promises.json with sentence-regex anchors where an anchor miss emits ANCHOR-LOST as its own finding state, never a silent pass — A1 pipes the payload fixtures from benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/ (built by the guard-matrix item, hence the dependency) into guard_binder_immutability.py stdin; pass 4 schema-vs-validator duals over new small fixtures in benchmarks/fixtures/schema-contradictions/ (minimal shared_terms binder, backend-only item with UI keys, bogus-extra-key control, shipped doc-gardner example config) plus the already-vendored frozen gringotts binder consumed through its existing REGISTRY.json entry. Findings carry content-derived stable ids; resolutions move to an append-only resolved list with resolving commit hashes; output carries the probe-set id list and hash. Gate adapter benchmarks/probes/flow-spec-contradictions.py: stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), --target support and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. It subprocess-runs the uv command above, maps the findings JSON into the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"}, exits 0 whether pass or fail, and reports status "fail" (fail-closed) if uv or the runner cannot run (uv is part of the host toolchain per design_facts; jsonschema resolves from the local uv cache). The ~7 seeded open findings are the baseline product, not failures: status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file (a new open finding on the intersection of probe ids, per the card's intersection rule); the first committed run under benchmarks/results/flow-spec-contradictions/ is that baseline. Baseline mechanics: during a gate run the adapter writes the runner's --out to a temp path and never rewrites the committed baseline; the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file; adapter metrics carry {open_total, open_on_intersection, probe_set_hash}; the committed <version>.json is written deliberately at release time — <version> resolves to the plugin.json version of the tree it is committed on (2.21.0 for the first baseline, written by the builder); the first committed baseline must contain the seeded findings this contract names, and the probe fails closed if a seeded finding is absent from its own first baseline. The oracle's gate run also emits benchmarks/results/gate/<date>-gate.partial.json and a temp runner output — both are transient verification artifacts, discarded, never committed. The card's results: frontmatter (benchmarks/flow/results/) contradicts the procedure's pinned --out path; this item follows the procedure text and leaves the frontmatter untouched (cards are edited only to flip probe_status), recording the mismatch as a permanent card-inconsistency finding in the runner output. Registry manifest (data for the composing registry item, which alone writes benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json): fixture files created = every file under benchmarks/fixtures/schema-contradictions/ (one registry entry per file); intended used_by = flow-spec-contradictions.

assertions: passes 1, 3, and 4 reproduce their seeded findings on the live repo (dead in-progress vocabulary, accepted missing from REF_STATES, between-waves/missing-repair-path, shared_terms schema/validator disagreement, UI-fields-on-non-UI dual acceptance) with content-derived stable ids; pass 2 reports zero live findings today

in --self-test, a fixture hooks.json with a typo'd event name yields a MISSPELLING finding, an unrecognized name yields UNKNOWN-EVENT distinct from findings, and an anchor whose sentence regex no longer matches emits ANCHOR-LOST
the A1 promise probe consumes the hooked-repo payload fixtures and asserts the immutability guard's deny exit

run_gate --only reports a valid contract row with {open_total, open_on_intersection, probe_set_hash} in metrics and status pass on the recorded baseline; a synthetic new finding in self-test flips status to fail
the card's probe_status frontmatter reads implemented

work_items.title: Finish the pack integrity suite so pins, prefixes, and drift are all machine-checked
work_items.summary: The existing thin pack-format probe grows into the full static suite: pin derivation replayed from the documented matching rule, prefix collision detection, overlay drift classification against upstream history, and a committed exception list that records the standing ruling for the one file that is reference data rather than a pack.
work_items.contract: Completes benchmarks/sme/sme-pack-static-suite.md: extends the existing thin benchmarks/probes/sme-pack-static-suite.py into the composer over the new benchmarks/sme-static/ scripts, so the probe declares partial: false and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips partial -> implemented. New files, each stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies) and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary (--self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise): benchmarks/sme-static/match_pins.py (implements the karta-plan SKILL sme matching steps 1-4, embeds the sha256 of the SKILL.md matching-rule section text — the hashed span runs from that section's heading line to the next same-level heading, LF-normalized — and exits 2 on mismatch — 'matching rule changed — re-verify implementation, update hash'; self-test fixtures include the templ vs github.com/a-h/templ whole-token non-match and a doctored-section-text case exercising the hash check), benchmarks/sme-static/cross_prefix.py (prefix->basename map over _shared plus overlays; collision or foreign PREFIXES use fails), benchmarks/sme-static/seed_drift.py (overlay vs every historical blob of the built-in: IDENTICAL / UPSTREAM-UNPROPAGATED / LOCAL-ADDITIVE / DIVERGENT, plus the dead-token and overbroad-token audit reusing the match_pins matcher), benchmarks/sme-static/consumers.json (enrolled consumer repo names, resolved as sibling directories of the --target repo's parent: parchmark, gringotts; each run records the SHA probed), and benchmarks/sme-static/exceptions.json (skip-list with one-line justification per entry, carrying the platform-native ruling from spec open_questions[4]: skip encoded in the exception file, no frontmatter added to shipped prompt text). Consumer repos are addressed through --consumers <path,...>, defaulting to those sibling directories — no absolute paths anywhere in code or configuration; a missing consumer directory is a clear error in live mode and irrelevant in self-test. Two deliberate card-layout deviations (the run.sh row recorded in benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md, shipped by a sibling item): (1) run.sh is not shipped — the gate probe is the single composer (minimalism); (2) the findings JSON lands at benchmarks/sme/results/<delivery-date>-sme-pack-static-suite.json per the card frontmatter results dir — the procedure step-1 sme-static/findings/ subdir is not created. Probe behavior: PROBE 1 runs validate_packs.py over built-ins (exceptions applied) and each enrolled repo's .karta/sme/, plus scripts/check_shared_copies.py for mirror parity, plus cross_prefix; PROBE 2 emits the per-binder pin table {binder, era, pinned, expected_contract, expected_coverage, underivable_pins, missing_pins} over the enrolled consumer repos' binders only (karta's own .karta/binders/archive/ is excluded from the denominator) with era partitioning and the coverage-mode unmatched-ecosystem list kept as a diagnostic; PROBE 3 runs seed_drift. All self-test fixtures (templ non-match, synthetic four-class overlays, doctored section text) are embedded in the scripts — no fixture files land under benchmarks/fixtures/. Gating and evidence split: gating = self-test fixtures plus karta-repo-owned surfaces; live consumer runs = recorded evidence. Status stance: karta-repo-owned zero-target surfaces (validator failures after exceptions, mirror drift, prefix collisions) and a match_pins rule-hash mismatch flip status to "fail" directly (fail-closed); an enrolled consumer directory that is missing is a clear error in live mode (status "fail" with a finding naming the path); consumer-side findings (expected at plan time, 2026-07-17, recorded not asserted: gringotts go-htmx DIVERGENT, dead-token templ, overbroad-token go, pin non-conformance) are findings under status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file, with the first committed findings JSON under benchmarks/sme/results/ as baseline. Bootstrap: when no prior findings JSON exists under benchmarks/sme/results/, consumer-side findings are recorded but do not flip status; that run's committed findings JSON becomes the baseline for all subsequent runs. Baseline mechanics: on every run the probe writes its dated findings JSON to benchmarks/sme/results/<delivery-date>-sme-pack-static-suite.json (overwriting a same-date file); the builder replaces <delivery-date> with the actual run date in file names at build time (the placeholder never appears in a committed filename); the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file; the builder commits the first run's file as the touched results artifact; the first committed baseline must contain the seeded findings this contract names, and the probe fails closed if a seeded finding is absent from its own first baseline. Emits the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"} on stdout, exit 0 whether pass or fail, and must fit run_gate's 120s probe budget. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item.

oracle.command: python3 benchmarks/sme-static/match_pins.py --self-test && python3 benchmarks/sme-static/cross_prefix.py --self-test && python3 benchmarks/sme-static/seed_drift.py --self-test && python3 benchmarks/probes/sme-pack-static-suite.py --self-test && python3 benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py --only sme-pack-static-suite
assertions: match_pins self-test covers the four matching steps and the templ non-match, and a doctored SKILL-section text makes it exit 2 naming the re-verify instruction
exceptions.json carries platform-native.md with its one-line justification and the built-in pack sweep reports 0 findings after exceptions

seed_drift classifies a synthetic overlay fixture into each of the four classes in self-test, and the live run records consumer-side findings (expected at plan time: gringotts go-htmx DIVERGENT, dead-token templ, overbroad-token go) without gating on them
the pin table partitions eras, counts sme-less binders as excluded not failing, and still emits the unmatched-ecosystem diagnostic list
run_gate --only reports partial false with a valid contract row; mirror drift or a prefix collision injected in self-test flips status to fail
the card's probe_status frontmatter reads implemented

work_items.title: Check the docs' numbers, links, and promises against what the repo actually contains
work_items.summary: One stdlib runner recomputes the repo's true counts and surfaces every documentation claim through a committed adjudication file, so a wrong number, dead link, unregistered label, or promised-but-unexecuted check shows up as a per-lane violation instead of prose drift.
work_items.contract: Implements benchmarks/parity/parity-doc-truth-ledger.md lanes B-G in full as benchmarks/probes/parity-doc-truth-ledger.py, so the probe declares partial: false and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips planned -> implemented. NOTE for review: the card's procedure names the runner benchmarks/probes/doc_truth.py while its frontmatter names benchmarks/probes/parity-doc-truth-ledger.py; the probe lands at the frontmatter/gate name only (one file, no wrapper) — a card erratum recorded, with the claims-file erratum below, in benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md (shipped by a sibling item). Stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), --target support, and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. Card erratum 2, flagged not silently applied: step 1's nonzero-exit-on-any-violation is superseded by step 10's regression-only gate and by run_gate.py's contract (the runner reads stdout JSON and ignores exit codes) — the probe exits 0 after emitting valid contract JSON regardless of status, nonzero only on crash. Truth vars recomputed each run (N_agents, N_skills, writer split, plugin version). Lane B binds every count-claim regex hit to an entry in the new committed benchmarks/claims.yaml — authored in the JSON subset of YAML so the stdlib probe parses it with json (the card names .yaml and stdlib has no YAML parser; also in the errata ledger) — with unadjudicated-claim, failed-expect, and stale-entry verdicts, including the literal-2 entries that keep the two locally-true sentences from false-positiving; the count-claim matcher tolerates up to three intervening hyphenated-adjective tokens between the number and the noun (so 'two READ-ONLY gate agents' and 'two READ-ONLY agents' are hits — card step 3's regex is illustrative, the seed observation is normative). Lane C stale major-version pins outside the archival allowlist; Lane D skill discoverability; Lane E ledger seeds DG-SCHEMA, KAIZEN-USERREPO, HOOKS-MATCHER-PARITY; Lane F relative-link existence; Lane G phase-label root registry (unregistered roots, wrong doc-path rows, duplicate leaves). claims.yaml and the ledger are add-only; retirement requires a retired: reason field with retired counts reported. All self-test fixtures are synthesized at runtime inside the self-test — no fixture files are committed. Emits the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"} with the per-lane vector in metrics (never a scalar sum), exit 0 whether pass or fail. The ~10 seeded violations are the baseline product: the first committed benchmarks/findings/doc-truth-2.21.0.json is the baseline and the probe reports status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file (any lane count above its last committed value); with no prior file it records the baseline and passes. Baseline mechanics: the probe re-emits its findings JSON on every run and the file is byte-deterministic — no timestamps, run dates, or shas — so re-running on an unchanged tree rewrites the committed baseline byte-identically; the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file; the first committed baseline must contain the seeded findings this contract names, and the probe fails closed if a seeded finding is absent from its own first baseline. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item.

assertions: the live run reproduces the seeded per-lane violations ('three agents' x2, marketplace 'two read-only gate agents' x2, the Karta 1.19 pins, undiscoverable karta-status, DG-SCHEMA and KAIZEN-USERREPO reds, the 2 unregistered label roots, and the README heading pair: 'The five skills' vs 10 skills, 'The two agents' vs 4 agents) with the two pinned locally-true sentences bound to literal-2 expects and NOT violated
an unbound count-claim hit self-test fixture yields unadjudicated-claim and a claims entry whose line-regex no longer matches yields stale-entry
metrics carry the per-lane vector and never a scalar sum; retired entries are counted visibly
run_gate --only reports partial false, status pass on the committed baseline; a synthetic lane regression in self-test flips status to fail
the card's probe_status frontmatter reads implemented

work_items.title: Drill the mirror machinery on the failure modes it silently ignores today
work_items.summary: Three rehearsals in throwaway copies of the repo show whether an executable bit survives projection, whether a degraded lock entry makes the sync destroy a third-party skill, and whether tampering with installed skill content is ever noticed.
work_items.contract: Completes benchmarks/parity/parity-mirror-sync-integrity.md by implementing P1-exec-bit-projection, P2-lock-degradation-survival, and P3-external-hash-liveness — the probe's own not_implemented list — inside the existing benchmarks/probes/parity-mirror-sync-integrity.py (python, not the card's sketched bash runner; same drills, single file — recorded in benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md, shipped by a sibling item), so the probe declares partial: false and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips partial -> implemented. The two existing wrapped checks (check_shared_copies, validate_plugin) stay. SAFETY INVARIANT (the card's own): every drill runs against its OWN fresh scratch copy (mktemp + rsync -a --exclude .git of the --target repo) and every script invocation addresses the copy's path ($S/scripts/...) — ROOT resolves from __file__, so a cwd-relative invocation would run the P2 rmtree drill destructively against the real repo; the probe asserts the copy path before each invocation and refuses to run the drills if the copy setup failed (fail-closed as a probe: setup failure reports status fail with a finding, never a silent skip). Fixture derivation per the card: first external mirror skill name, else the synthesized bench-external-probe lock entry, keeping the P2/P3 denominator alive. P1 chmod +x on a canonical skill script, run write-mode sync in the copy, assert owner-exec on both projections; P2 delete computedHash from the entry, run write mode, PASS iff the external skill dir survives; P3 append a byte to the external SKILL.md, PASS iff --check or validate_plugin flags it by name. Evidence lands in benchmarks/parity/results/ per the card frontmatter results: field and README section 5, deliberately overriding Procedure step 8's benchmarks/results/ literal (card-internal conflict resolved in favor of frontmatter, declared here; the card text is left untouched — cards are edited only to flip probe_status). All three fail at seed (0/3): per the card, probe IDs are permanent and a previously-passing ID may never regress — the probe reports status "fail" only on regression against the last committed results file, with the first committed benchmarks/parity/results/<delivery-date>-sync-probes.json (per-probe evidence cards: command outputs, stat modes, exit codes) as baseline; evidence cards normalize scratch paths (each drill's copy root is replaced with the literal $S) so a same-date re-run produces a byte-identical evidence file. Baseline mechanics: on every run the probe writes its dated evidence JSON to benchmarks/parity/results/<delivery-date>-sync-probes.json (overwriting a same-date file); the builder replaces <delivery-date> with the actual run date in file names at build time (the placeholder never appears in a committed filename); the regression baseline is the newest git-tracked results file for this vector (git ls-files), never an untracked or same-run file; when no tracked baseline exists (the first build run) no regression is possible, status is pass, and that run's committed evidence file becomes the baseline; the first committed baseline must contain the seeded findings this contract names, and the probe fails closed if a seeded finding is absent from its own first baseline. Emits the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"}, exit 0 whether pass or fail; the rsync copies and both sync invocations must fit run_gate's 120s probe budget. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item. Self-test: stdlib-only argparse, PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary (--self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise), driving the three drills against a miniature synthetic tree, never the real repo — the synthetic tree embeds the repo's real sync scripts (the drills invoke $S/scripts/sync_codex_skills.py, with P3 falling back to validate_plugin.py) and includes a zero-external-lock variant so the bench-external-probe synthesis path is exercised; self-test also covers the fail-closed copy-setup guard (refusal when the scratch copy is missing) and the baseline-regression flip (a previously-passing probe ID flipping to false yields status fail).

work_items.title: Make a version bump impossible to commit without a matching green benchmark run
work_items.summary: The repo's commit gate learns one new rule: if the staged change bumps the plugin version, there must be a committed green gate file for exactly that version and exactly this commit's parent, or the commit is blocked with the fix named.
work_items.contract: Extends scripts/hooks/precommit_gate.py (the dev-repo PreToolUse Bash hook) with the release block from benchmarks/meta/release-gate-blocking-smoke.md step 3; the card's retroactive past-release audit probe is explicitly deferred (recorded in benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md, shipped by a sibling item), so its probe_status frontmatter stays planned and the card is not touched — the deferred audit, when built, must compare a gate artifact's recorded sha against the tagged commit's PARENT, since the artifact records the pre-bump sha. Detection: when the command is a git commit (existing detector), additionally check whether the commit would change the .claude-plugin/plugin.json version: compare the version fields of git show HEAD:.claude-plugin/plugin.json and git show :.claude-plugin/plugin.json — the staged blob — plumbing only; a diff is never parsed. When the commit command carries -a/--all (or a pathspec that could include .claude-plugin/plugin.json), the version comparison reads the WORKING-TREE .claude-plugin/plugin.json against git show HEAD:.claude-plugin/plugin.json instead of the staged blob — the commit will record working-tree content in those forms. Only a version change arms the block. Sha semantics, pinned: the gate artifact records the sha it ran on; when armed, the block accepts the commit iff a green full-gate file exists — some benchmarks/results/gate/*-gate.json (never a *.partial.json — subset runs do not count) — whose plugin_version equals the new version, whose recorded karta_sha equals the HEAD the bump commit is being created on (the gate ran on the pre-bump tree, after the working-tree version edit, before any commit), and whose summary shows fail == 0 and error == 0; skipped vectors deliberately do not block (SKIPPED stays loud in the gate file and run_gate's own exit is non-strict — an all-skipped-but-green gate file admitting the commit is design, not oversight). The block additionally requires that gate file to be staged in this commit (its path appears in git diff --cached --name-only; working-tree presence counts under -a); a green matching file left unstaged blocks with a reason naming git add <file>. Operator sequence, stated for the release checklist: (1) finish the RC tree and edit the working-tree plugin version; (2) run python3 benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py — the dated artifact records the current HEAD sha; (3) git add the gate artifact together with the version bump and create the bump commit on that same HEAD, so the artifact is committed with the bump and its recorded sha equals the bump commit's parent — this dissolves the first-release chicken-and-egg (no seed gate file is needed before the first gated release). Missing, red, partial-only, malformed, version-mismatched, sha-mismatched, or unstaged gate files block with exit 2 and a reason naming the exact fix — a gate failure is a block, not an internal error. The hook's existing stance is unchanged: internal errors (unreadable stdin, malformed payload, unexpected exceptions) still fail OPEN, the KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 escape hatch still applies and is named in the reason, and the five existing gates run exactly as before. Self-test extends the embedded suite with fabricated staged-diff states and fabricated gate files (green match staged, green match unstaged, red, sha mismatch, version mismatch, partial-only, absent, malformed JSON — malformed gate JSON blocks, since an unreadable verdict is not a green verdict; plus -am and pathspec bump cases proving the block arms on working-tree content) using the existing stubbed-runner pattern, keeping the [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary format (--self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise). README.md's existing sentence describing this block (line ~121 of benchmarks/README.md) becomes true rather than aspirational; no doc edit needed.

assertions: a commit with no plugin.json version change never arms the block and behaves exactly as before (all pre-existing self-test checks still pass)

a version bump blocks when the gate file is missing, red, a .partial.json only, malformed, version-mismatched, sha-mismatched, or unstaged — each with a reason naming the run_gate command (or the git add) and the escape hatch

work_items.title: Mine what a delivery really costs and whether sessions ran the scripts the prose demands
work_items.summary: A transcript miner reads the recorded session files to report per-item token and wall-clock medians by agent type, and checks whether each mandated helper script was actually invoked when the rules said it must be — with sessions it cannot measure counted out loud.
work_items.contract: Implements modes 1 (cost) and 4 (compliance) of benchmarks/perf/perf-delivery-telemetry.md — the card's own two-pass rollout order; modes 2 (timeline) and 3 (oracle-count) are deferred — so the probe declares partial: true and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips planned -> partial. Ships the card-named benchmarks/perf/mine_sessions.py <transcript-dir>... --since <date> --out <file>: stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), pure JSONL parsing, no judge; it also accepts --binders <path>..., and when omitted derives each consumer's .karta/binders/ plus archive/ from the transcript dir's encoded project path. Mode 1 per the card: delivery windows from the deliver Skill invocation, per-subagent usage summed from the agent's own JSONL (meta carries no usage), builder/gate classification including karta:-scoped forms, builder-to-item mapping via binder item ids with unmapped spawns in an explicit unattributed bucket never dropped, per-item medians and overhead_ratio with numerator and denominator emitted separately, models per agentType. Mode 4: the four compliance rows (scan_secrets before commits, validate_binder before first worktree creation, check_shared_terms after qualifying waves, karta_next --footer before session end) with transcript-counted denominators, and sessions with missing or truncated subagent files reported as an unmeasurable count, never silently excluded. Every miner output carries the card's step-7 context block (project, binder slug, plugin version, item count + estimate mix, models per agentType, claude-code version if present). Because live transcript dirs decay (~30d cleanup), correctness rests on a committed synthetic fixture transcript at benchmarks/perf/fixtures/miner-transcript/ (small hand-authored JSONL session + subagents + meta files exercising every classification branch, plus binder.json — a minimal binder whose item ids match all but one builder meta description) driven by the self-test — the miner is validated against the fixture, and live mining is best-effort over whatever raws survive. Gate adapter benchmarks/probes/perf-delivery-telemetry.py accepts run_gate's --target <repo-root> invocation (default: its own repo root, matching the two existing probes) in addition to --self-test, and maps the fixture-transcript run plus the live-dir summary into the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"}, exit 0 whether pass or fail. Gating and evidence split: gating = the fixture run (fixture-run mismatch = status "fail", fail-closed on the miner's own correctness); live = recorded evidence — each gate run's live pass writes a well-formed dated snapshot to benchmarks/perf/results/<delivery-date>-perf-telemetry.json summarizing whatever live transcript dirs survive (content recorded, never asserted), and the builder commits the first snapshot; the builder replaces <delivery-date> with the actual run date in file names at build time (the placeholder never appears in a committed filename). All live-dir mining is error-contained — any parse or IO exception during the best-effort live pass increments the unmeasurable/skipped count in metrics and never crashes the probe or affects status; absent or decayed live transcript dirs are an unmeasurable count with status "pass" (decay is an expected environment fact the card documents, not a probe defect). Host precondition, referenced not owned: the card's cleanupPeriodDays>=365 setting in ~/.claude/settings.json is host configuration outside this binder (see scope.excluded); decay before it is set simply lands in the unmeasurable count. Self-test prints [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item. Registry manifest (data for the composing registry item, which alone writes benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json): fixture files created = every file under benchmarks/perf/fixtures/miner-transcript/ including binder.json (one registry entry per file); intended used_by = perf-delivery-telemetry.

work_items.title: Start the running account of what keeping all this machinery costs
work_items.summary: An append-only ledger gains its first row — machinery counts, manual release steps, bench spend, triage load, and the recorded tool-choice resolution — and a small script recomputes and diffs a fresh row against the last one each release.
work_items.contract: Implements benchmarks/meta/ownership-cost-ledger.md in full, so the probe declares partial: false and the card's probe_status frontmatter flips planned -> implemented. Ships the card-named benchmarks/field/ownership_ledger.py: stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies) and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. Row parts per the card: (a) machinery counts (sync/projection scripts, byte-identical mirrored copies and duplicated bytes over the check_shared_copies scope plus the codex projections — the three skill mirrors are inside check_shared_copies scope, so this compression loses no card coverage — hook scripts, bench runner scripts and LOC), (b) manual release steps not enforced by the release block — enumerated from the latest docs/releases/v*-checklist.md minus what scripts/hooks/precommit_gate.py enforces, countable only once the release block exists in the tree (hence the dependency on the release-block item); the host-side cleanupPeriodDays setting counts as one such manual step, (c) bench spend summed from whatever gate artifacts and quarterly results exist under benchmarks/results/ — where artifacts carry no wall-clock or token fields, part (c) records "n/a (not yet instrumented)" per honesty rule 12, never 0; the sum activates as spend fields appear in later artifacts, (d) triage load (findings opened vs resolved vs waived across committed vector results), plus one buy-vs-build row per hand-rolled component citing benchmarks/research/evaltool-2026-07-17.json with a yes/no still-correct and a one-line reason. Ships benchmarks/field/results/ownership-ledger.json with row 1 recording the already-written resolution from benchmarks/results/2026-07-17-baseline.md: promptfoo ADOPT for the executable arms only (pinned via benchmarks/bench/package.json when that lane lands, not before; the deterministic lane never depends on node) and bayes_evals only at k>=5 with raw counts at n<=3. The ledger lands at benchmarks/field/results/ownership-ledger.json per the card procedure; the card frontmatter's results: benchmarks/meta/results/ is a known card-internal inconsistency — left untouched (cards are edited only to flip probe_status) and recorded as a finding. Two modes, pinned: --append writes the new row (release-time only); the default --check mode recomputes the fresh row in memory and diffs it against the last committed row WITHOUT writing — the gate adapter and this item's oracle use check mode only, so a gate run leaves ownership-ledger.json byte-identical; self-test exercises append/refusal behavior on temp copies only, never on the committed ledger. The script refuses to rewrite or reorder existing rows (it only appends, verifies rows are date-ordered and release-unique, and diffs the fresh row against the last committed one); any buy-vs-build row answered no-longer-correct without an action or dated waiver is a violation. Gate adapter benchmarks/probes/ownership-cost-ledger.py accepts run_gate's --target <repo-root> invocation in addition to --self-test and maps the check-mode row diff into the gate probe JSON contract {"id","status":"pass"|"fail","partial","implemented_checks","findings","metrics"}, exit 0 whether pass or fail. Gating and evidence split: gating = self-test plus ledger-integrity checks; the fresh-row diff itself is recorded in the gate row's metrics — evidence for the human, never asserted. Stance: a corrupted ledger (out-of-order or duplicate rows) or an unactioned no-longer-correct row reports status "fail"; growing cost deltas are findings for the human, never a gate failure. The <date>-gate.partial.json written by the oracle's run_gate --only invocation is a transient verification artifact — never committed by this item.

work_items.title: Record every new test fixture in the shared registry with its fingerprint
work_items.summary: After the fixture-writing items land, one composing step appends a fingerprint entry for each new fixture file to the shared registry so later audits can prove nothing changed unnoticed.
work_items.contract: Composes the registry entries for every fixture shipped by this binder, as the single writer of benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json — no other work item touches that file. Input: the registry manifest clause that ends each of the six fixture-writing items' contracts in .karta/binders/bench-probe-buildout.json (flow-guard-enforcement-matrix, flow-spec-contradictions, dark-status-surface-probes, sec-untrusted-input-surfaces, field-delivery-state-audit, perf-delivery-telemetry); each clause names the exact fixture paths that item creates and the used_by list it intends, stated as data for this item. Ships benchmarks/fixtures/update_registry.py: stdlib-only argparse with a PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies) and a --self-test mode printing [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an N/N checks passed summary; --self-test exits 0 only when the summary is N/N checks passed, nonzero otherwise. For each manifested fixture file on the assembled tree it computes sha256 and APPENDS an entry {id, path, sha256, used_by} to benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json — append-only: existing entries are never edited, reordered, or removed; entries are per-file, so a directory of payloads gets one entry per file (e.g. every benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/payloads/*.json). The used_by lists are carried verbatim from the manifests. The script's --check mode re-reads benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json and verifies every listed fixture path exists relative to the repo root and its sha256 matches the file on disk, exiting nonzero on any missing path or mismatch; --self-test drives append, duplicate-refusal, and mismatch detection against temp registry copies, never the committed file.

assertions: every fixture path named by the six registry manifest clauses has exactly one appended entry whose sha256 matches the file on the assembled tree, with the manifested used_by lists carried verbatim
pre-existing registry entries are byte-identical to their state before this item ran

work_items.title: Write down every place the build deliberately departs from a benchmark card
work_items.summary: A committed ledger records each known deviation between what a vector card says and what actually shipped, with the reason, so card text and code can be reconciled deliberately instead of drifting apart.
work_items.contract: Ships benchmarks/meta/card-errata-2026-07-17.md: a plain-language markdown table with one row per known deviation, columns {card, card_text, implemented_as, why}. Rows to record (each cited by the affected sibling item's contract): (1) flow-guard ref-namespace spelling — the card's refs/karta/<slug>/built/<id> names a namespace that does not exist; the real namespace is refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/built, used by guard_delivery_stop.py and karta-build. (2) flow-guard probe filename — card step-3's benchmarks/flow/probe_guards.py is superseded by the probes/<vector-id>.py convention run_gate.py composes. (3) doc-truth runner naming — the card procedure's benchmarks/probes/doc_truth.py lands as the frontmatter/gate name benchmarks/probes/parity-doc-truth-ledger.py, one file, no wrapper. (4) doc-truth claims file — benchmarks/claims.yaml is authored in the JSON subset of YAML so the stdlib probe can parse it. (5) mirror-sync implementation language — python inside the existing probe, not the card's sketched bash runner; same drills. (6) sme suite run.sh — not shipped; the gate probe is the single composer. (7) release-gate retroactive audit — the card's past-release audit probe (benchmarks/probes/release-gate-blocking-smoke.py) is deferred and its probe_status stays planned while the blocking portion ships in the precommit hook; when built, the audit must compare a gate artifact's recorded sha against the tagged commit's parent, since the artifact records the pre-bump sha. Cards themselves are not edited by this item at all, and by sibling items only to flip their own probe_status frontmatter (binder scope unchanged); this ledger is the reconciliation record. (8) dark-status results path — card step 8's benchmarks/results/<version>-status-truth.json lands as benchmarks/dark/results/<delivery-date>-status-truth.json per the card frontmatter results dir, the README family-local-evidence rule, and the sibling probes' date-keyed convention.
